subtopic: Patterns dominant in 2025-2026 (post-Anthropic Sept 2025 context-engineering era)

source.title: Anthropic Engineering blog (Sept 2025 - Apr 2026), Simon Willison's blog, cross-references

framing: This catalog covers patterns that became dominant after Sept 2025, when Anthropic published 'Effective context engineering for AI agents' and reframed the discipline. Companions: llm-patterns-eugene-yan (2023 baseline), llm-patterns-anthropic (Dec 2024 workflows/agents), llm-patterns-12-factor-agents, llm-patterns-2024-2026-additions (transitional). This is the current state-of-the-art.

patterns.name: Context Engineering
patterns.summary: The named discipline of curating what enters an LLM's finite context window each step. Supersedes prompt engineering as the primary engineering activity for production agents.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic — Effective context engineering for AI agents (Sept 29, 2025)

patterns.name: Context Rot
patterns.summary: Named phenomenon: as token count grows, model recall and reasoning quality degrade. Attention budget is finite (n^2 pairwise relationships); training data is biased toward shorter sequences.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Sept 2025; needle-in-haystack benchmarks

patterns.name: Compaction
patterns.summary: When context nears its limit, summarize the conversation and restart with the summary. The first lever for long-horizon agents. Claude Code uses message history + 5 most-recent files.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Sept 2025

patterns.name: Structured Note-Taking / Agentic Memory
patterns.summary: Agent writes notes to external files (NOTES.md, TODO.md) that persist outside the context window and are pulled back when needed. Demonstrated by Claude playing Pokemon tracking thousands of game steps.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Sept 2025; Claude Sonnet 4.5 memory tool

patterns.name: Just-in-Time Context
patterns.summary: Don't pre-load all data. Carry lightweight identifiers (file paths, queries, URLs) and load data dynamically via tools when needed. Mirrors human cognition (we use filesystems, not memorization).
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Sept 2025

patterns.name: Progressive Disclosure
patterns.summary: Layered loading: metadata first, then summary, then full content. Same principle drives Agent Skills (3-level), Code Mode (filesystem tool discovery), and just-in-time retrieval.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Sept-Oct 2025

patterns.name: Context Plumbing
patterns.summary: Framing context as having sources (where it appears: emails, files, sensors) and sinks (where the AI runs). The engineering work is moving context between sources and sinks.
patterns.source_anchor: Matt Webb — interconnected.org (Nov 2025)

patterns.name: Reinforcement (in agent loops)
patterns.summary: On every tool call, return not just the result but also reminders of the overall objective and current task state. Claude Code's TODO list does this implicitly.
patterns.source_anchor: Armin Ronacher — Agent design is still hard (Nov 2025)

patterns.name: The Agent Loop (modern definition)
patterns.summary: Simon Willison and Anthropic converged: 'agents are LLMs autonomously using tools in a loop.' This is the simplest accurate definition as of 2025.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Sept 2025 (refining Dec 2024 definition); Simon Willison

patterns.name: Workflows vs Agents (the spectrum)
patterns.summary: Workflows = LLMs+tools orchestrated through predefined code paths. Agents = LLMs dynamically directing their own processes. Anthropic's rule: start with workflows; reach for agents only when justified.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic — Building effective agents (Dec 2024), refined Sept 2025

patterns.name: Single Agent with Tools (ACI design)
patterns.summary: One agent, a loop, 5-12 well-described tools. The default for most enterprise use cases. Quality of the agent-computer interface (ACI) dominates outcomes.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Dec 2024 + Microsoft 2026

patterns.name: Multi-Agent Orchestration (production)
patterns.summary: Lead agent + specialized subagents in parallel. Token usage explains 80% of performance variance on BrowseComp. Justified for valuable, parallelizable, broad-context tasks. NOT for coding (too many dependencies).
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic — How we built our multi-agent research system (Jun 2025)

patterns.name: Sub-Agent Architectures for Context Isolation
patterns.summary: Specialized sub-agents work in clean context windows on focused tasks. Each may use tens of thousands of tokens but returns only 1-2K of distilled summary to the lead. Achieves separation of concerns at the context level.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Sept 2025

patterns.name: Self-Directed Agent Swarms
patterns.summary: Model spawns up to 100 sub-agents with up to 1,500 tool calls, swarm topology automatically created without predefined subagents or workflow. Demonstrated by Kimi K2.5 (Jan 2026).
patterns.source_anchor: Kimi K2.5 release (Jan 2026)

patterns.name: Coding Agents (as agent specialty)
patterns.summary: Specialized agents for software development. Patterns: plan-then-act, read-grep-edit loops, test-driven inner loops, patch-based output, sandbox execution. Examples: Claude Code, Cursor Agent, Codex, Aider, Cline, Devin.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Claude Code best practices (Apr 2025); ecosystem 2025-2026

patterns.name: Computer Use / Browser Use Agents
patterns.summary: Agents that operate GUIs via screenshots + synthesized mouse/keyboard, not via APIs. Useful for software without APIs. Slow, brittle, security-sensitive — best in sandboxes.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Computer Use (Oct 2024); OpenAI Operator; Google Project Mariner

patterns.name: Generative Agents (memory-stream architecture)
patterns.summary: Park et al. 2023's pattern (memory stream + retrieval scoring on recency/importance/relevance + reflection + planning) — still the reference architecture for simulation/social agents.
patterns.source_anchor: Park et al. 2023 (Stanford Smallville)

patterns.name: Maker-Checker / Generator-Verifier (agentic)
patterns.summary: Generator agent produces output; verifier agent checks it via tools/rules/different model. Loop until verifier accepts. Now standard for safety-critical deployments (legal, medical, financial).
patterns.source_anchor: Microsoft orchestration patterns (2026); Anthropic Evaluator-Optimizer (Dec 2024)

patterns.name: Agent Skills (SKILL.md format)
patterns.summary: A skill = folder with SKILL.md (YAML frontmatter: name, description) + optional bundled scripts/instructions/resources. Three-level progressive disclosure: metadata -> SKILL.md body -> bundled files.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic — Equipping agents for the real world with Agent Skills (Oct 16, 2025)

patterns.name: Code Execution with MCP (Code Mode)
patterns.summary: Agent writes code that calls MCP tools instead of making direct tool calls. 98.7% token reduction in Anthropic's reference example (150K -> 2K). Tools discoverable via filesystem; data can stay in execution environment.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic — Code execution with MCP (Nov 4, 2025); Cloudflare 'Code Mode' (independent)

patterns.name: AGENTS.md convention
patterns.summary: Repo-level convention for agent instructions (like README but for agents). Donated by OpenAI to the Agentic AI Foundation in Dec 2025.
patterns.source_anchor: OpenAI; AAIF (Dec 2025)

patterns.name: Eval-Driven Development (formalized)
patterns.summary: Build evals to define planned capabilities BEFORE the agent can fulfill them. When a new model drops, the suite reveals which bets paid off. Anthropic's explicit methodology.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic — Demystifying evals for AI agents (Jan 9, 2026)

patterns.name: Grader Type Taxonomy (Code/Model/Human)
patterns.summary: Three grader kinds, combined per task: code-based (deterministic, cheap, brittle), model-based (LLM-as-judge, scalable, needs calibration), human (gold standard, used to calibrate others).
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Jan 2026

patterns.name: pass@k vs pass^k metrics
patterns.summary: pass@k = at least 1 of k attempts succeeds; pass^k = all k succeed. For customer-facing agents pass^k is the real bar. 75% per-trial -> pass^10 = 6% (consistency is hard).
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Jan 2026 (formalized); origin pass@k = Codex paper 2021

patterns.name: Capability vs Regression Evals
patterns.summary: Capability evals start at LOW pass rate (a hill to climb). Regression evals stay near 100% (catch backsliding). Capability evals 'graduate' to regression suite when saturated.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Jan 2026

patterns.name: End-State Evaluation
patterns.summary: Don't grade the path the agent took; grade the final state (DB row, file, API state). Allows agents to find creative valid paths. Especially for state-mutating agents.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic multi-agent research Jun 2025; Jan 2026 evals post

patterns.name: Eval Saturation
patterns.summary: When score hits 100% on solvable tasks, signal dies. SWE-bench Verified: 30%->80% in one year, now near saturation. Need to retire saturated evals or add harder tasks.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Jan 2026

patterns.name: Eval Awareness (alignment concern)
patterns.summary: Frontier models can detect when they're being evaluated and behave differently. Now a real alignment/eval-design problem. Anthropic Opus 4.6 BrowseComp paper made this explicit.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic — Eval awareness in Claude Opus 4.6 (Mar 2026)

patterns.name: LLM-as-Judge with Calibration
patterns.summary: Use LLM graders for scalability, but periodically calibrate against expert humans. Use structured rubrics, isolate dimensions per call, give the LLM an 'unknown' escape.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Jan 2026 (refined from Yan 2023 G-Eval / Vicuna)

patterns.name: Containment / Agent Blast Radius
patterns.summary: Architectural assumption: the agent WILL do something wrong eventually. Cap what it CAN do, not what it WILL do. Egress allowlists, filesystem jails, network policies, capability scoping.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic — How we contain Claude across products (Featured 2026)

patterns.name: Lethal Trifecta
patterns.summary: The dangerous combination = (untrusted input) x (access to private data) x (ability to exfiltrate). An agent with all three is a prompt-injection bomb. Mitigate by removing any one of the three.
patterns.source_anchor: Simon Willison — coined 2024

patterns.name: Egress Allowlisting
patterns.summary: Agent can only make outbound HTTP to a declared list of domains. Used by Claude Cowork. Important caveat: own provider's API can become an exfil channel (Prompt Armor attack Jan 2026).
patterns.source_anchor: Claude Cowork release (Jan 2026); Prompt Armor exfil analysis

patterns.name: Browser as Sandbox
patterns.summary: Use the browser's 30-year-hardened sandbox: <iframe sandbox>, CSP headers, WebAssembly in Web Workers, File System Access API. Paul Kinlan's Co-do reference implementation.
patterns.source_anchor: Paul Kinlan (Google) — 'the browser is the sandbox' (Jan 2026)

patterns.name: Auto Mode / Permission Boundaries
patterns.summary: Pre-declared boundaries the agent is allowed to act within, rather than per-action confirmation prompts. Claude Code's 'auto mode' lets agents skip permissions inside their declared scope.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic — How we built Claude Code auto mode (Mar 25, 2026)

patterns.name: Brain-vs-Hands Decoupling (Managed Agents)
patterns.summary: Separate the brain (LLM reasoning + planning) from the hands (tool execution, file I/O, network). Run them in different processes/containers/regions. Enables independent scaling, security, observability.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic — Scaling Managed Agents: Decoupling the brain from the hands (Apr 8, 2026)

patterns.name: Rainbow Deployments (for stateful agents)
patterns.summary: Gradually shift traffic from old to new agent versions while both run simultaneously. Critical because long-running agents can be anywhere in their process when a new version ships.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic multi-agent research Jun 2025

patterns.name: The 'think' Tool
patterns.summary: An explicit tool the agent can call to pause and reason without taking action. Different from extended thinking — this is a deliberate scratchpad mid-loop. Surprisingly effective for complex tool-use scenarios.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic — The 'think' tool (Mar 20, 2025)

patterns.name: Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents
patterns.summary: Patterns: durable execution, resumable state, regular checkpointing, deterministic retry wrappers around non-deterministic LLM calls, idempotent tool calls.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic — Effective harnesses for long-running agents (Nov 26, 2025); Harness design (Mar 24, 2026)

patterns.name: Prompt Caching as Central Primitive
patterns.summary: Cache the long static prompt prefix (system, tools, RAG context). 90% cost / latency reduction on cache hits. Claude Code: 'we build our entire harness around prompt caching.'
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Aug 2024; OpenAI Oct 2024; Gemini; Thariq Shihipar (Anthropic Feb 2026)

patterns.name: Tool Result Clearing
patterns.summary: Cheapest form of compaction: once a tool has been called and its result consumed, clear the raw result from later context. Now a Claude Developer Platform feature.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Sept 2025; Claude Developer Platform feature

patterns.name: Self-Improving Tool Descriptions
patterns.summary: A tool-testing agent uses a flawed MCP tool, then rewrites the tool description to avoid the failures it encountered. Anthropic measured 40% reduction in task completion time.
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic multi-agent research Jun 2025

patterns.name: Memory Tool (file-based persistent memory)
patterns.summary: Native file-based memory primitive: agents read/write to persistent files outside context. Released with Claude Sonnet 4.5 (2025).
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Sonnet 4.5 launch (2025)

patterns.name: Model Context Protocol (MCP) — de facto standard
patterns.summary: Open standard for connecting LLMs to tools/data sources. Launched Nov 2024, now thousands of servers, SDKs for all major languages, donated to Agentic AI Foundation (Dec 2025).
patterns.source_anchor: Anthropic Nov 2024 launch; AAIF stewardship Dec 2025

patterns.name: Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF)
patterns.summary: Linux Foundation umbrella for agentic AI (Dec 9, 2025). Founding platinum members: AWS, Anthropic, Block, Bloomberg, Cloudflare, Google, Microsoft, OpenAI. Stewards: MCP, AGENTS.md, goose.
patterns.source_anchor: Linux Foundation announcement Dec 9, 2025

see_also: llm-patterns-eugene-yan.json — 2023 baseline (Evals, RAG, Fine-tuning, Caching, Guardrails, Defensive UX, Feedback)
llm-patterns-anthropic.json — Dec 2024 Workflows + Agents framing (still the foundational mental model)
llm-agent-components-weng.json — 2023 component decomposition (Planning, Memory, Tool use)
llm-patterns-2024-2026-additions.json — transitional catalog (mostly superseded by this file)
